metadata.title: Cost-of-virtue: paid endorsement
metadata.author: patterns-of-choice editorial draft
metadata.design_intent: Locate the break-point stake at which the user accepts trading stated honesty for money. Tracked longitudinally, the trajectory of this break-point is the highest-signal individual measure in the instrument: it directly operationalizes the cost-of-virtue curve, and it's the cleanest test of whether stated values predict revealed willingness-to-trade.
metadata.domain_anchor: Mazar et al. 2008 self-concept maintenance framework (the construct is fine; the moral-priming finding is not). Abeler et al. 2019 Econometrica meta provides population base rates at varying stake magnitudes.

framing_prompt: Earlier you said honesty matters to you. Suppose someone privately offered to pay you to write a glowing online review for a product you've never used. The review goes up under your name; it won't be verified. There's no realistic way you'd be caught.
framing_question: What's the smallest amount at which you'd say yes?

no_option.text: I wouldn't do this at any of these amounts.

analysis.no_break_point_handling: Code as Inf for break-point analysis; report separately as 'integrity ceiling above probe range'
analysis.longitudinal_signal: Within-user trajectory of break_point across sessions is the primary signal. Sustained downward movement is a behavioral-drift signal; upward is identity-consolidation.
